title: The One Where Eddie Won't Go

jokes.text: What are you doing here? - Watching you sleep.
jokes.setup: Chandler finds Eddie in his apartment unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Eddie casually admits to watching him sleep
jokes.explanation: Strong opener that immediately establishes Eddie's unsettling behavior. Perfect economy and character-specific creepiness.

jokes.text: It makes me feel, um, peaceful.
jokes.setup: Why would someone watch another person sleep?
jokes.punchline: Eddie's sincere, peaceful explanation makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Eddie's genuine delivery makes the creepy behavior even funnier through contrast.

jokes.text: Hannibal Lecter? Better roommate than you.
jokes.setup: Chandler needs to emphasize how bad Eddie is as a roommate
jokes.punchline: Comparing him unfavorably to a fictional cannibal
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference with perfect escalation. Chandler's sarcastic voice shines through.

jokes.text: What about the other nights when you don't see me, huh?
jokes.setup: Eddie defending his behavior
jokes.punchline: Implies he's been watching every night, just hiding better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the situation exponentially worse. Eddie's logic is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: Last night you went and got some water... and I was nice enough to hide behind the door.
jokes.setup: Eddie explaining his 'considerate' behavior
jokes.punchline: Frames stalking as being 'nice enough' to hide
jokes.explanation: Masterful inversion where Eddie presents stalking as a favor. The specific detail makes it horrifyingly funny.

jokes.text: Where'd you hear it before?
jokes.setup: Eddie wants to hear 'I want you out' from Chandler's lips
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically asks where Eddie heard it before
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler snark that cuts through Eddie's dramatic moment.

jokes.text: Finally got that time machine working, huh?
jokes.setup: Someone is wearing an obviously dated outfit
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests they traveled from the past
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with Chandler's signature delivery style.

jokes.text: A mirror?
jokes.setup: Joey asks what he doesn't have regarding his hat purchase
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests he lacks self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comeback. Monica's practical nature shines through.

jokes.text: Cachet? Jaunty? Chandler gave me word-of-the-day toilet paper.
jokes.setup: Joey using unusually sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's getting words from novelty toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - explains his vocabulary while staying true to character. Great absurd detail.

jokes.text: And the wind can make us goddesses. Do you know who takes our wind? Men. They just take it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining a self-help book's metaphors
jokes.punchline: Her earnest delivery of ridiculous wind-stealing concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe buying into new-age nonsense with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: That sounds kind of cool. It's like The Hobbit.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to relate to the wind-keeper book
jokes.punchline: Comparing feminist empowerment to Tolkien fantasy
jokes.explanation: Ross's intellectual but tone-deaf attempt to connect. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: It is nothing like The Hobbit.
jokes.setup: Ross's inappropriate comparison
jokes.punchline: Rachel's flat rejection of his analogy
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Ross's misguided enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Hello. Who wants one of my phallic-shaped man-cakes?
jokes.setup: Gunther offering pastries
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reframes innocent offer through her book's lens
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the wind-keeper theme, turning innocent pastries into feminist commentary.

jokes.text: Do I have lipstick on my teeth?
jokes.setup: Agent tells Joey to look at her after he asks if she's mad
jokes.punchline: She's more concerned about appearance than his career crisis
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the agent's priorities. Classic Hollywood self-absorption.

jokes.text: Take any job you can get, and don't make on the floor.
jokes.setup: Agent giving career advice about a dog act
jokes.punchline: The advice is literally about house-training
jokes.explanation: Great absurd escalation comparing Joey's career to a dog act.

jokes.text: This book could have been called Be Your Own Wind Keeper, Rachel!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is deeply invested in this self-help book
jokes.punchline: She thinks it was written specifically about Rachel
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's earnest over-identification with pop psychology. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Actually, it's the movie theater that has the timetable.
jokes.setup: Rachel accusing Ross of controlling their schedule
jokes.punchline: Ross literally explaining movie theater logistics
jokes.explanation: Ross's pedantic response to Rachel's metaphorical complaint. Very in character.

jokes.text: You go, girl!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to support Rachel's feminist awakening
jokes.punchline: Using an outdated catchphrase awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Monica's awkward attempt at trendy support. The immediate regret sells it.

jokes.text: I can't pull that off, can I?
jokes.setup: Monica's failed attempt at being hip
jokes.punchline: Her immediate self-aware recognition of the failure
jokes.explanation: Monica's self-awareness about her failed coolness attempt is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: You know I don't... have a problem with that.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks how she can grow if Ross won't let her blow
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward pause before addressing the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Relies on innuendo but Ross's uncomfortable delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: Sorry. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying not to apologize as part of her empowerment
jokes.punchline: Immediately apologizing for apologizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Rachel's struggle with new empowerment concepts.

jokes.text: This is why I don't date women who read.
jokes.setup: Ross confused by Rachel's wind-keeper book behavior
jokes.punchline: Blames literacy rather than the specific book's silliness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misogynistic exaggeration from the intellectual character. Great economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Envelope one of two.
jokes.setup: Ross opens his VISA bill
jokes.punchline: There's a second envelope, implying massive debt
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective escalation setup. The dread builds nicely.

jokes.text: The guy said they suited me. He spoke with an accent. I was confused.
jokes.setup: Joey spent $3500 on ceramic animals
jokes.punchline: Blames salesman's accent for his poor financial decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - the accent somehow justified the expense. Great character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: Joey, you owe $1100 at 'I Love Lucite.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Joey's debt problems
jokes.punchline: The store name reveals another frivolous purchase category
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with a ridiculous store name that suggests more bad purchases.

jokes.text: I don't need you getting judgmental and condescending and pedantic. - Toilet paper? - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Joey using sophisticated vocabulary to criticize Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler correctly guesses it's from the word-a-day toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that pays off the earlier toilet paper joke while advancing the argument.

jokes.text: What I do know is you owe $2300 at 'Isn't It Chromantic?'
jokes.setup: More of Joey's debt being revealed
jokes.punchline: Store name plays on 'romantic' with chrome theme
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay in the store name continues the escalation of Joey's ridiculous purchases.

jokes.text: Maybe because the last one was made by Pepperidge Farm.
jokes.setup: Eddie says new goldfish is feistier than the last one
jokes.punchline: Reveals the previous 'fish' was actually a cracker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Eddie's delusions. Perfect brand reference and Chandler's deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Your last roommate's kidney? That's a tomato.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks about something Eddie is dehydrating
jokes.punchline: Eddie casually mentions kidney before correcting to tomato
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing Eddie moment. The casual delivery of something horrifying followed by mundane correction.

jokes.text: I used to be Brice on All My Children.
jokes.setup: Gunther relating to Joey's soap opera career troubles
jokes.punchline: Reveals Gunther's acting past and dramatic death
jokes.explanation: Great character development for Gunther. Explains his understanding of Joey's situation.

jokes.text: This is not out of the blue! This is smack-dab in the middle of the blue!
jokes.setup: Eddie says Chandler's eviction demand is 'out of the blue'
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates the metaphor to emphasize obviousness
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler escalation with creative metaphor extension. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Five-O dollars.
jokes.setup: Joey correcting his pronunciation in audition
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by saying 'Five-O' like the police show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - trying to sound professional but making it worse. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You were going for bridge there, weren't you?
jokes.setup: Joey mispronounces 'bridge' as 'budge'
jokes.punchline: Director calls out the obvious mistake sympathetically
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment showing Joey's audition failure. The pity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You totally let him wash his feet in the pool of your inner power. And his puppet too!
jokes.setup: Using wind-keeper book metaphors to discuss Monica's relationship
jokes.punchline: Extending the metaphor to include the puppet
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the wind-keeper metaphor. Including the puppet in the spiritual violation is absurd and funny.

jokes.text: Only because you took up half the circle!
jokes.setup: Monica defending her spin-the-bottle victory
jokes.punchline: Rachel attacks Monica's size to discredit the win
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their argument with a character-specific insult about Monica's past weight.

jokes.text: You are such a leaf blower!
jokes.setup: Monica using wind-keeper book terminology as insult
jokes.punchline: Adapts the wind metaphor into a specific gardening tool insult
jokes.explanation: Creative extension of the wind metaphor into modern technology. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: Judas is a little loose.
jokes.setup: Joey warning about his 3-D Last Supper artwork
jokes.punchline: Unintentional religious/sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Simple innuendo works because of Joey's innocent delivery and religious context.

jokes.text: You're living the dream!
jokes.setup: Joey didn't get the audition
jokes.punchline: Ross enthusiastically celebrates Joey's failure as success
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal from Ross who's completely misreading the situation.

jokes.text: Uh, it was an impulse buy. Near the register.
jokes.setup: Joey spent $1200 on a plastic bird
jokes.punchline: Treats massive purchase like buying gum at checkout
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - minimizing a huge expense as an impulse purchase. Great character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: There is no alley behind Macy's.
jokes.setup: Eddie claims he got mannequin head from alley behind Macy's
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact geographic correction
jokes.explanation: Chandler's deadpan fact-checking of Eddie's obviously made-up story works well.

jokes.text: We'll make it a chip chick.
jokes.setup: Eddie wants to use mannequin head as chip bowl
jokes.punchline: Creates portmanteau of 'chip' and 'chick'
jokes.explanation: Eddie's disturbing wordplay fits his character. The pun makes the macabre suggestion worse.

jokes.text: We took a road trip to Las Vegas! Oh, sweet Moses!
jokes.setup: Eddie completely fabricates shared memory with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Peak Eddie delusion moment. The fake memory is elaborate and Chandler's reaction is perfect.

jokes.text: Is anyone else starting to really like him?
jokes.setup: Eddie's behavior has been increasingly disturbing
jokes.punchline: Ross finds the delusion endearing rather than alarming
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection. Ross's reaction is the opposite of what audience expects, making it funny.

jokes.text: I guess I got the wrong apartment.
jokes.setup: Eddie confronted with evidence he doesn't live there
jokes.punchline: Accepts false reality rather than face truth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant resolution to Eddie's arc. His complete acceptance of false reality is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Goodbye, you fruit-drying psychopath.
jokes.setup: Eddie finally leaves
jokes.punchline: Chandler's specific farewell references Eddie's dehydrator obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler send-off combining his sarcasm with specific callback to Eddie's weird habits.

jokes.text: What happened to the foosball? It's a cantaloupe.
jokes.setup: They're about to play foosball
jokes.punchline: Eddie dehydrated the foosball into fruit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual punchline that caps Eddie's dehydrator obsession. Perfect absurd logic.

jokes.text: Is he housetrained or will he leave little bathroom tiles all over?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Joey's ceramic dog
jokes.punchline: Bathroom tiles instead of normal dog waste
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting ceramic material to waste products. Very Chandler-esque wit.

tmdb_overview: Creeped out by Eddie's behavior, Chandler demands he leave but Eddie refuses. Joey faces financial troubles; the women have a 'goddess meeting.'
